name: Split Pea Soup
tagline: Slow-cooked split pea soup with carrot, ham hock, and thyme
highlight: Pantry-cheap, dense protein, no eggs ever

hero.alt: Thick split pea soup with ham and croutons
hero.caption: split pea — thick and savory

healthFacts: Split peas pack ~16g protein and 16g fiber per cooked cup.
A ham hock adds collagen-rich gelatin during the slow simmer — gut-friendly.

steps.title: Sweat the mirepoix
steps.detail: Cook onion, carrot, celery in 2 tbsp olive oil 10 minutes until soft. Add garlic, cook 1 minute.
steps.tip: The trinity of soup. Don't rush it — soft veg = smoother soup.

steps.title: Add peas and hock
steps.detail: Stir in split peas, ham hock, bay, thyme. Pour in water/stock. Bring to a boil, then drop to a low simmer.
steps.tip: Rinse split peas first to remove any grit.

media.alt: Peas and ham hock in pot
media.caption: add peas and hock

steps.title: Long simmer
steps.detail: Cover and simmer 70 minutes, stirring occasionally, until peas have broken down and soup has thickened.
steps.tip: Lid askew — fully covered traps too much steam, fully open thickens too much.

steps.title: Pull hock, salt, finish
steps.detail: Pull the hock out, dice the meat off the bone, return meat to pot. Discard bay. Salt to taste — wait until the end since the hock is salty.
steps.tip: Soup will thicken further on cooling — thin with stock if reheating.

media.alt: Finished split pea soup with ham
media.caption: pull hock, salt, finish
